key_insight: A coupled CEL simulation plus ANN surrogate can accurately predict weld temperature, axial force, and defect formation in HDPE friction stir welding, enabling fast identification of safe process windows.
engineering_relevance: The framework supports process optimization for thermoplastic joining by reducing trial-and-error and highlighting conditions that improve weld quality and reduce wormhole defects.
method: 3D Coupled Eulerian-Lagrangian (CEL) model for thermo-mechanical FSW behavior
Two multilayer perceptron ANNs trained on experimental data for temperature and axial-force prediction
Process-map generation across rotational speed, travel speed, and bottom preheating conditions
evidence_signal: The ANN models achieved R2 values of 0.91 to 0.99, and the CEL model matched experiments well for void formation, thermal response, and mechanical response.
watch_item: The surrogate and process maps are trained and validated on a limited experimental parameter space, so extrapolation to new tool geometries, materials, or broader operating windows should be done cautiously.
paper_title: Computational and machine learning modelling approaches for weld quality predictions in friction stir welding of high-density polyethylene
